title: Automation Order
excerpt: Is there any way to choose which order the automations trigger? I have over 30 automations that need to trigger in a certain order before moving the pulse to a different board.

posts.author: GrantDC (Grant)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there any way to choose which order the automations trigger? I have over 30 automations that need to trigger in a certain order before moving the pulse to a different board.

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @GrantDC - sadly no. At this time there is no way to order the trigger of automations on a board. This has been brought forward to monday as a feature request in the past.

Mark

posts.author: andrewalmand (Andrew Almand)
posts.cookedHtml:
@GrantDC

While there is no way to force Monday automations to go in a certain order, I can think of two possible workarounds.

  1. Add in a Status column that controls the start of automations. Build the changing of this status column into your automations so its the final step, which in turn activates the next automation.
    -Your first automation would start this chain, and then the next automation would change the status to the next step, starting the next automation and so on and so on.

  2. Utilize Integromat or Zapier. You can replicate most automations within those apps and you could easily build a 30 automation scenario that runs everything in order. This method gives you a lot more control over everything, conditional options and can integrate other applications. The downside is learning how to set it up if you haven’t already and/or dealing with error handlers as 30 scenarios is a lot of room for something to mess up.

If you need help setting up either of these options feel free to reach out.

posts.author: Christophe (SC)
posts.cookedHtml:
The benefit of the Automations and even integrations is really reduced whern they are depending one from another but can’t be triggered in the correct order.

A new feature, affecting both, Automations and Integrations should be added to let us define the needed execution order. This could be a kind of dependency model where we could D&D the automations or integrations from a list and put them one after the other or in parallele.

It is very sad to get an email where there is no “Creator” as the assignment of the Creator is triggered after the sending of the email.
